REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT CITE ANY AUTHORITY SUPPORTING AN IIED CLAIM BROUGHT BY THE FORECLOSED UPON OWNER WHO DID NOT USE THE PROPERTY AS HIS HOME/ABODE/DWELLING, ETC. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR WRONGFUL EVICTION AND TRESPASS IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY STATES ALL ELEMENTS OF THESE CAUSES OF ACTION, INCLUDING PLAINTIFF'S RIGHT TO POSSESSION AND POSSESSION OF THE PROPERTY VIA SOMEONE ELSE WITH NO INDEPENDENT CONTRACTUAL POSSESSORY RIGHTS OF THEIR OWN. E.G. A TENANT REMAINS IN POSSESSION OF THE PROPERTY VIA A SUBTENANT; A PERSON IS SUBJECT TO POST-FORECLOSURE EVICTION IF THEY ARE THE FORMER TRUSTOR OR A PERSON CLAIMING UNDER SUCH FORMER TRUSTOR; BUT NOT IF THEY ARE A TENANT. CCP 1161A ; CCP 1161(1); SFRO. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Appearances: Jonathan D. Fink of Wright Finlay & Zak, LLP (949.477.5050) for defendant and moving party; Nelson W. Goodell of the Goodell Law Firm (415.495.3950) for plaintiff and responding party; Coby N. Keller of Keller & Weber (949.553.0450) for John T. Lacy, trustee and Glenn and Carrie Donahue, trustees.

The court rules on plaintiff's objections (filed June 20, 2025) to Erick Feitshan's declaration as follows: overruled as to objections 1 - 2, 4 - 6. Sustained as to objection 3. Defendant's objections (filed June 30, 2025) to the King, Smith, Walker and Chandler declarations are ruled on as follows: King declaration - overruled as to paragraphs 12, 21 - 25, 34, 38, 40, 43, 46, 49 - 50, 54, 56 - 57, sustained as to paragraphs 17, 27, 30 - 32, 36 - 37, 39, 45, 55, paragraph 52 is sustained in part and overruled in part. Objections to the Smith declaration are all overruled. Objections to the Walker declaration are overruled. Objections to the Chandler declaration are overruled as to paragraphs 2, 7, 13, 18 and sustained as to paragraph 9.

The matter is argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED, in part, and DENIED in part. As a preliminary matter, the Court ruled on the parties' evidentiary objections, although the parties did not submit proposed orders on the objections.

Summary adjudication is DENIED as to causes of action 1, 2, 5, 6, and 7. As too all 5 of these causes of action, there are triable issues of fact regarding whether the Notice of Default correctly and lawfully stated the amount of money owed. Defendant's primary evidence that they excluded pre-maturity default interest consists of a one-page worksheet with little elaboration as to its contents, which does not shift the burden. As to negligent misrepresentation and fraud, there are triable issues of fact regarding whether P acted in reliance on the Notice of Default in that P was unable to secure the money that was purportedly due. Regarding slander of title, this cause of action need not rely on Defendant's recordation of the Notice of Default. There are triable issues of fact in whether Defendants acted with malice or reckless disregard of the truth when recording the Trustee's Deed Upon Sale given the Notice of Default may have overstated the amount of money due and evidence that Defendant refused Plaintiff's attempts to pay.

Summary adjudication is GRANTED as to causes of action 8 and 9. Defendant has shifted the burden. Plaintiff has no competent evidence that Val-Chris was involved in post-foreclosure conduct. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Olivier J. Labarre; Wright, Finlay & Zak LLP; 949-477-5050; appearing as counsel for defendant Val-Chris Investments, Inc.
Coby N. Keller; Keller & Weber; 949-553-0450; appearing as counsel for defendants John T. Lacy and Clenn and Carrie Donahue.
Nelson W. Goodell; The Goodell Law Firm; 415-495-3950; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Andrew King.

Mr. Labarre contests the portions of the court's tentative decision overruling the Demurrer to the cause of action for Trespass and Wrongful Eviction. Mr. Goodell contests the portion of the tentative ruling relating to plaintiff's claim for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Plaintiff does not cite any authority supporting an IIED claim brought by the foreclosed upon owner who did not use the property as his home/abode/dwelling, etc. Demurrer to the causes of action for wrongful eviction and trespass is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently states all elements of these causes of action, including Plaintiff's right to possession and possession of the property via someone else with no independent contractual possessory rights of their own. E.g. a tenant remains in possession of the property via a subtenant; a person is subject to post-foreclosure eviction if they are the former trustor or a person claiming under such former trustor; but not if they are a tenant. CCP 1161a ; CCP 1161(1); SFRO.
